title: Lemma expand: vector inequality implication and per-crosscut assembly

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Location:** problem.tex, Lemma `expand`.

The polarization step ($2V\!\cdot\!W=\|V\|^2+\|W\|^2-\|V-W\|^2$, giving $V\!\cdot\!W<V'\!\cdot\!W'$) is correct. Remaining gaps:

1. **Prove the implication** actually used: the text says $\|(1-s)V+sW\|<\|(1-s)V'+sW'\|$ for $s\in(0,1)$ 'is true provided $V\!\cdot\!W<V'\!\cdot\!W'$'. Write the proof: expand $\|(1-s)V+sW\|^2=(1-s)^2\|V\|^2+s^2\|W\|^2+2s(1-s)\,V\!\cdot\!W$ and compare term-by-term using all three hypotheses $\|V\|<\|V'\|$, $\|W\|<\|W'\|$, $V\!\cdot\!W<V'\!\cdot\!W'$ (all are needed; verify strictness and that no term has the wrong sign).
2. **Assemble per-triangle into per-crosscut.** The squeeze inequality is per-triangle. A crosscut may traverse several $\mathcal C$-triangles. Show that within each traversed triangle the crosscut sub-segment runs from the distinguished vertex toward the distinguished edge so the squeeze hypotheses apply, and that strict arclength increase is additive across triangles, giving $\ell(f(C))>\ell(C)$ for the whole crosscut.
